transcript: What is a solute?

target.reply: Well, think of it like when you mix sugar into water to make it sweet. The sugar is called a solute because it dissolves in the water!
A solute is something that gets mixed up and disappears in a liquid. Like when you see salt vanish in water, the salt is the solute!
Imagine you're a magician, and your magic trick is to make things disappear in water. When you put something like cocoa powder in milk and it disappears, that cocoa powder is what we call a solute.
It's like when you're making lemonade. The sugar you stir in the water until you can't see it anymore? That's the solute - it's the part that mixes in and goes invisible.
Have you ever helped in the kitchen and mixed flour into soup to thicken it? The flour mixes into the soup without being seen. That flour is called a solute because it blends into the liquid.
You know how you can add food coloring to icing to change its color? The food coloring mixes in and becomes part of the icing. That food coloring is a solute, because it dissolves into the icing.

Imagine if you had a secret invisible ink that only shows up when you mix it with water. When that ink spreads out and disappears in the water, we call the ink a solute because it dissolves in the liquid.

Let's think about hot cocoa. Before you stir, you see the cocoa powder on top. After stirring, it's all mixed in and you can't see it anymore. The cocoa powder is the solute because it blends into the milk.

So, if you're making a potion and add glitter to your potion to make it sparkle, the glitter that mixes in the liquid is what we would call a solute. It's fun to see it spread out and vanish into the potion!
When you're painting and mix a color into the paint to make a new color, the color you added is like a solute. It mixes all the way in to create something new and exciting!
